eventMetadata.context_description: Hungary's parliamentary election on April 12 saw Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party suffer a landslide defeat to Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party, which secured a supermajority and prompted Orbán's immediate concession, ending his 16-year tenure as prime minister. Orbán further relinquished his parliamentary seat on April 25, solidifying his exit from power and driving trader consensus to a 98.4% implied probability that he will be the next listed leader out before 2027, far ahead of stable incumbents like Starmer or Netanyahu. While the decisive margin minimizes risks, potential challenges include legal disputes over results, recounts, or transitional delays in government formation, though these face significant barriers given the opposition's dominance.

markets.question: Will Khamzat Chimaev be the next fighter to be ranked first in the UFC Pound-For-Pound Rankings in 2026?

If Islam Makhachev remains #1 in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings through December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the Pound-For-Pound rankings within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Islam/No Next #1 in 2026”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Ilia Topuria at 47.5% implied probability to become the next UFC pound-for-pound #1 in 2026, driven by his rapid rise after capturing the lightweight title with a first-round knockout of Charles Oliveira at UFC 317 in June 2025, solidifying his #2 P4P ranking and fueling recent callouts for a superfight with Islam Makhachev. The Islam/None in 2026 outcome at 39% reflects Makhachev's entrenched #1 status as undefeated welterweight champion following his UFC 322 defense against Jack Della Maddalena, with his next bout slated for August amid recovery. Khamzat Chimaev's 32% stake ties to his flawless 15-0 record and middleweight title win over Dricus du Plessis at UFC 319, heightening anticipation for his UFC 328 main event defense versus Sean Strickland this weekend. Petr Yan's 25.9% nods to reclaiming bantamweight gold against Merab Dvalishvili at UFC 323, while former middleweight king Dricus du Plessis (23%) and welterweight contender Della Maddalena (20%) linger as dark horses based on stylistic edges and comeback potential.

markets.question: Will Lee Jung-hyun win the 2026 Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election?
markets.description:
The Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker mayors will not count.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Min Hyung-bae at 97.7% implied probability to win the June 3, 2026, inaugural Jeonnam-Gwangju Integrated Special City mayoral election, reflecting the Honam region's longstanding status as a Democratic Party stronghold where opposition People Power Party candidates historically poll below 20-30% amid high progressive turnout. Min, a sitting National Assembly member from Gwangju, unified the Democratic nomination in mid-April after a competitive runoff primary victory over Jeollanam-do Governor Kim Young-rok, consolidating party support without internal fractures. PPP's Lee Jung-hyun, former party chair with regional ties, recently proposed debates on integration and economic issues but faces structural barriers in this deep-blue battleground. Upsets would require a major Min scandal, national political shifts, or anomalous voter mobilization before election day.

markets.question: Will Katie Porter win the California Governor Election in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to according to the candidate who wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election currently scheduled for November 3, 2026.

If the results of the election are not confirmed by July 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: With California's June 2 nonpartisan top-two primary looming, trader consensus pins Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer as near-dead-even frontrunners at 39% and 39% implied probabilities, reflecting a fragmented field where Democrats dominate voter registration but face Republican surges from Steve Hilton and Chad Bianco in recent polls like Emerson's April survey. Becerra's momentum stems from post-debate polling gains—jumping to 24% in Gudelunas (April 23-27)—bolstered by his attorney general experience and Eric Swalwell's withdrawal, while self-funding billionaire Steyer holds steady via climate activism and endorsements. High undecideds (23%+) and GOP top-two risks keep odds tight; late ad blitzes, final debates, or Newsom endorsements could tip the balance for advancement.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Wellington be 14°C on May 6?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Wellington Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 6 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Wellington Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/nz/wellington/NZWN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 37.5% implied probability for a 17°C high in Wellington on May 6, edging out 18°C at 27.5% and 16°C at 18%, reflecting tight uncertainty in MetService's latest forecast of a 16°C peak at Kelburn under strengthening northerlies advecting warmer air from the north. Recent settled conditions and northerly flow have delivered above-climatological highs—averaging 14–15°C in May—with yesterday's 15.9°C observation underscoring potential for model upside, as ensembles like NZLAM show a 16–18°C spread influenced by cloud variability and evening shower timing. Watch MetService's next 9am update and May 5 afternoon observations for shifts in peak heating potential.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on EUR/USD's potential to breach key 2026 levels hinges on persistent monetary policy divergence, with the Federal Reserve holding the fed funds rate at 3.50%-3.75% amid resilient U.S. growth, contrasting the ECB's main refinancing rate at 2.15% following its March policy stasis. Eurozone inflation accelerated to 3% in April—highest since late 2023—while economic sentiment plunged to 93.0 and unemployment held at 6.2%, underscoring regional weakness. Spot EUR/USD trades near 1.169 as of May 5, with forecasts clustering around 1.16-1.20 amid mixed analyst outlooks. Watch the FOMC meeting May 6-7 for rate signals, U.S. nonfarm payrolls May 8, and Eurozone Q1 GDP flash May 12 for catalysts that could sway market-implied paths.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-13 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: New York's 13th Congressional District, encompassing heavily Democratic Upper Manhattan neighborhoods with high renter populations, overwhelmingly favors Democrats, reflected in trader consensus pricing the Democratic Party at 93.5% to win the November 3, 2026, general election. Incumbent Rep. Adriano Espaillat, first elected in 2017 and reelected decisively in 2024, bolsters this position amid a lack of credible Republican challengers, as no major GOP candidate has emerged. Recent developments center on the June 23 Democratic primary, where four challengers—including Darializa Avila Chevalier, who polls competitively in internals—filed against Espaillat, but victory by any Democrat secures the seat per historical patterns in this solid D-rated district. Scenarios challenging this include a primary upset producing an unelectable nominee, late GOP recruitment of a high-profile contender, or unforeseen scandals, though barriers remain high given the partisan lean.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest 9-day forecast, updated at 11:30 HKT on May 5, projects a maximum temperature of 22–27°C on May 6 under a moderating northeast monsoon, with mainly cloudy skies, light rain patches early, and bright periods later amid east force 4 winds. This range aligns with trader consensus favoring 27°C (40.5% implied probability) and 26°C (32.5%), below May's typical 28–29°C highs due to cooler monsoon air suppressing daytime heating. Key differentiators include cloud cover extent—persistent overcast capping at 26°C, while extended bright periods could push toward 27–28°C—and 70–90% humidity limiting convective warming. HKO updates multiple times daily; observations through May 6 will refine exact outcomes at the Observatory station.

Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 8 at 2:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Deportivo La Coruña, entrenched in second place in LaLiga Hypermotion standings with 64 points from a strong run of wins and draws—including a 3-1 victory over Mirandés last weekend and a 1-1 draw at Burgos on April 25—enter Jornada 39 as promotion contenders against relegation-battling Cádiz CF, mired in 18th on 38 points. Cádiz's winless streak over five matches features heavy losses like 3-0 to Sporting Gijón and 3-0 at Real Valladolid, exacerbated by injuries to key attackers Javi Ontiveros (groin), Suso (muscle), and Bojan Kovacevic (knee). The earlier season 2-2 draw at Riazor highlights Cádiz's home fightback potential, but Deportivo's away form and table position drive trader sentiment toward the visitors.

markets.question: Will Lovable be acquired before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if credible reporting confirms that any entity enters into an agreement to acquire the listed company by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the "Which companies will be acquired before 2027?" market centers on Caesars Entertainment at an 80% implied probability, driven by March reports of exclusive $7 billion takeover talks with Fertitta Entertainment, and AI coding startup Cursor at 75% following SpaceX's April 21 announcement of a $60 billion acquisition option—either exercisable later this year or convertible to a $10 billion collaboration for xAI developer tools. Google's $32 billion Wiz cybersecurity deal completion in March exemplifies surging AI infrastructure M&A amid competitive positioning, boosting odds for Perplexity AI (28%) and GitLab (18%). Viking Therapeutics trades at 60% on big pharma interest in its VK2735 obesity drug ahead of phase 3 trials. Watch deal announcements and regulatory reviews as key catalysts before the December 31, 2026 resolution.
